key_insight: Riemann-normal coordinates make Levenberg-Marquardt updates more geometrically consistent by adding higher-order corrections that reduce parameterization-induced curvature effects at finite step sizes.
engineering_relevance: This can improve the stability and speed of nonlinear least-squares solvers used in regression, physics-informed neural networks, and large-scale model fitting.
method: Reformulate the LM step in a moving Riemann-normal coordinate frame.
Extend geodesic acceleration to arbitrary-order finite-step corrections.
Use line search along the RNC curve to control step length while preserving LM-like cost reduction.
evidence_signal: Across classical nonlinear least-squares benchmarks, a PINN failure-mode test, and a large-scale potential-energy-surface fitting task, RNC-LM improved convergence, reduced relative L2 error to about 1e-3, and delivered up to a 34-fold speedup.
watch_item: Higher-order geometric updates add implementation complexity and may require careful line-search and curvature handling to remain efficient and numerically stable.
paper_title: Higher-Order Geometric Updates for Levenberg-Marquardt Method via Riemann Normal Coordinates
